commit.message:
fix: use built-in context_validation in recipe YAMLs (#3753)

All 4 core recipes now declare context_validation rules:
- task_description: nonempty
- repo_path: git_repo

The Rust runner (0.2.12) validates these before executing any steps,
replacing the bash pre-flight scripts. Bumps MIN_RUNNER_VERSION.

Closes #3741

commit.message:
feat: generate complete data/uniques.json from full game data

Replace the hand-curated 66-item data/uniques.json with a fully
auto-generated version covering all 403 uniques in the game.

Add process_all_uniques.py which:
- Reads all uniques from exports_json/uniques.json
- Resolves base item names and implicits from items.json by subTypeID
- Formats affix ranges from raw mod values (min-max)
- Separates unique special effects into unique_effects[] from
  tooltipDescriptions (distinct from regular affixes)
- Handles level-scaling tooltip patterns like [1,c,5] (c = per level)
- Infers gameplay tags from mod tag fields
- Generates a slug key per item (deduplicated)

Also fix format_tooltip in process_set_items.py to handle the same
[1,c,5] scaling pattern (was crashing on non-numeric bracket content).

Update run_all.py to include process_all_uniques.py in the pipeline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Deploy: squad-deploy.yml created (auto-deploy to Pages on main merge)

feat: extract set items into data/set_items.json

Add process_set_items.py which reads the setItems array from
exports_json/uniques.json (already extracted by process_uniques.py)
and produces data/set_items.json with 47 items across 18 sets.

- Resolves base item names from items.json by subTypeID
- Formats affix ranges (e.g. '+6-12% Necrotic Penetration')
- Infers set names from common word patterns across item names
- Strips hidden mods (hideInTooltip) from affix list
- Formats set bonus text from tooltipDescriptions
- Includes a sets index mapping setId to member slugs
- Note: PlayerProperty / AbilityProperty affixes show raw internal
  names — these require manual curation for human-readable text

Also update run_all.py to include process_set_items.py in the
pipeline (runs after process_uniques.py).

Fix docker-compose.yml volume indentation bug that prevented the
backend and frontend services from starting with 'docker compose up'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: MaxSim re-ranker + 70/30 hybrid scoring in parser

Two-stage pipeline: BM25 top-50 retrieval → MaxSim re-ranking.
Consumes word_similarity.lua sparse matrix for semantic matching.
Implements D1 (70/30 weights) and D3 (MaxSim first) per Frink research.
Target: 93% accuracy (D4).

- maxsim_score(): ~20 LOC, sums best per-token similarities
- Stage 1: existing BM25 scoring with all validation gates (P1-P6)
- Stage 2: MaxSim re-ranks top-50, hybrid = 0.70*BM25 + 0.30*MaxSim
- Returns raw BM25 score externally for backward compatibility
- Stable sort preserves deterministic tie-breaking

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add write operations gated by ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE (#12)

* Add write operations gated by ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE

Implement the full deposit write workflow, all behind an explicit opt-in:

  ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE=true   (or '1') enables write tools
  ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE=false  (default) — write tools not registered

## New tools (only registered when write is enabled)

Deposition CRUD:
  create_deposition   POST /api/deposit/depositions
  get_deposition      GET  /api/deposit/depositions/:id
  update_deposition   PUT  /api/deposit/depositions/:id  (metadata JSON)
  delete_deposition   DELETE /api/deposit/depositions/:id (drafts only)

File management:
  upload_file             PUT to bucket URL; accepts utf-8 or base64 content
  delete_deposition_file  DELETE /api/deposit/depositions/:id/files/:file_id

Deposition actions:
  publish_deposition   .../actions/publish
  edit_deposition      .../actions/edit  (unlock published record)
  discard_deposition   .../actions/discard
  new_version          .../actions/newversion

## Safety design

- Write tools are not registered at all when ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE is false,
  so AI agents never see them unless the operator has opted in.
- Each handler also checks the flag independently (defence-in-depth).
- All write methods call requireAuth() — clear error if key is missing.
- docker-compose.yml exposes ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE (default: false).

## Tests (test/zenodo-write.test.ts, 19 tests)

- Gate: write tools absent in tool list when disabled
- Gate: write tools present when ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE=true
- Read-only tools still present when write is enabled
- 10 defence-in-depth handler tests (each write tool returns isError when flag off)
- 2 not-authenticated tests (write enabled, no API key)
- 4 live sandbox round-trip tests (create → upload → update → delete),
  activated only when ZENODO_SANDBOX_API_KEY is set

CI: passes ZENODO_SANDBOX_API_KEY secret and sets ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE
accordingly.

38/38 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review feedback: SSRF fix, type safety, encoding validation, upload size limit, description fix

fix: CREATURE-003/004/007/008 validation bugs in lint.py — closes GATE-4

All four bugs had the same root cause: rules were looking for drives
and states at the top-level fields instead of inside behavior sub-table.

- CREATURE-003: now checks behavior.drives emptiness (not behavior keys)
- CREATURE-004: now checks behavior.states for idle (not top-level states)
- CREATURE-007/008: now reads drives from behavior_t (not top-level fields)

Add bounds/all_versions search params, default community, and community tools (#11)

* Add bounds/all_versions search params, default community, and community tools

- Add ZENODO_COMMUNITY environment variable: sets a default community
  for all search_records calls when no communities param is given.
  Logged at startup; surfaced in get_auth_status and search_records
  results as default_community / community fields.

- Add bounds parameter to search_records: geolocation bounding-box
  filter forwarded verbatim to the Zenodo API
  (format: 'west,south,east,north' in decimal degrees).

- Add get_community tool: fetch title, description, curation policy
  and links for a community by its identifier.

- Add list_communities tool: search/browse Zenodo communities with
  optional query, page, size and sort parameters.

- Add test/zenodo-search.test.ts with 13 tests covering all new
  features; all 32 tests (19 existing + 13 new) pass.

- Document ZENODO_COMMUNITY in docker-compose.yml env block.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add authenticated CI tests using ZENODO_API_KEY secret (#10)

* Add authenticated CI tests using ZENODO_API_KEY secret

- Pass ZENODO_API_KEY secret to the test and summary steps in test.yml
- Add test/zenodo-auth.test.ts with 7 tests covering:
    * get_auth_status returns authenticated:true when key is set
    * list_depositions succeeds and returns an array
    * list_depositions respects the size parameter
    * list_depositions draft filter: all results have submitted=false
    * list_depositions published filter: all results have submitted=true
    * get_auth_status confirms the token is accepted by Zenodo's API
    * set_api_key via tool call then verified with get_auth_status
- All tests skip gracefully with a clear message when ZENODO_API_KEY
  is not available (local runs, fork PRs that cannot access secrets)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review comments on authenticated tests and workflow

- Fix incorrect comment: get_auth_status calls /api/me not /api/deposit/depositions
- Add graceful skip on network/Zenodo errors in list_depositions tests
- Remove ZENODO_API_KEY from test-summary step (not needed there)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/eic/zenodo-mcp-server/sessions/1d206272-c89a-4074-8c7f-976d1563f9b4

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove brittle submitted-field assertions in status filter tests

The Zenodo API can return depositions with submitted:true when filtering
by status=draft (e.g. new-version drafts of already-published records).
Replace per-deposition submitted assertions with simpler array checks
that only verify the API call succeeded and returned an array.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/eic/zenodo-mcp-server/sessions/2cfc7bf3-7aa3-4b22-9c2f-09e8c9ddbe48

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add playback controller hook and journal REST API endpoints

Add the time-lapse replay system for flywheel visualization (Enhancement 4/5):

- usePlaybackController hook: loads JSONL journals, replays events at
  variable speed via requestAnimationFrame, supports 7 speed tiers
  (0.5× to 100×), seek by position/iteration/act, step forward/backward,
  and speed-dependent batching (≥10× batch per frame, ≥25× act-transitions only)

- REST API endpoints in dashboard server:
  GET /api/runs — list recorded runs with journal metadata
  GET /api/runs/:id/journal — stream JSONL event journal
  GET /api/runs/:id/journal/index — return companion seek index

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: danielbdyer <23263526+danielbdyer@users.noreply.github.com>

Validate ZENODO_BASE_URL in ZenodoClient constructor (#8)

If ZENODO_BASE_URL is misconfigured (e.g. extra text appended through
a copy-paste error in the MCP config), the server previously threw an
opaque 'Invalid URL' error on every tool call that built a URL, while
get_auth_status silently succeeded. Now the constructor validates the
URL immediately at startup and emits a clear diagnostic message.

Added unit tests covering valid URLs, trailing-slash stripping, and
three invalid-URL cases (embedded whitespace, empty string, relative path).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add Event Journal Writer for flywheel time-lapse recording

Implement append-only JSONL recorder that subscribes to the pipeline
PubSub event bus, enriches events with sequence numbers, iteration
tracking, and flywheel act classification, then periodically flushes
to disk with a companion index file for random-access replay.

Key components:
- JournaledEvent type with sequence, iteration, and act enrichment
- JournalIndex/JournalIterationIndex/JournalActIndex for seek support
- deriveAct pure function (server-side mirror of stageToAct)
- subscribeJournalWriter following the subscribeWsBroadcaster pattern
- Periodic flush with 10MB safety cap
- Companion .index.json written alongside the .jsonl

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: danielbdyer <23263526+danielbdyer@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: courtyard-kitchen-door spawn review (#405)

Two spawn edges to wood-splinters are INTENTIONAL, not a duplicate bug.
The door has two break transitions (locked->broken, closed->broken) — each
spawns wood-splinters because breaking a wooden door produces splinters
regardless of starting state. locked requires strength 3, closed requires 2.

Added explanatory comment above transitions block so future edge analysis
does not re-flag this as a duplicate spawn.

Room wiring verified: door is correctly placed in courtyard.lua instances
and exits.east. No manor-kitchen room exists yet (Level 2 content), and
all states correctly have traversable = false.

Closes #405

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(v4): add cache refresh and fix cache key consistency in JsonSchema

Addresses corvus-dotnet/Corvus.JsonSchema#720:

- Add refreshCache parameter to all factory methods (FromText,
  FromStream, FromFile, FromUri, From) matching V5 API
- Standardize cache keys to always include alwaysAssertFormat
  flag (FromText/FromStream were using bare URI while other
  methods used uri__format pattern)
- Add second cache check after extracting canonical URI from
  document , avoiding expensive Roslyn compilation when
  the schema is already cached
- Add BuildCacheKey helper for consistent key generation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add FastAPI backend with ACLED/GDELT integration and severity scoring (#2)

* Add FastAPI backend with ACLED/GDELT integration and severity scoring

- ACLED API service with OAuth authentication for conflict event data
- GDELT API service for contextual news articles with tone analysis
- Composite severity score algorithm (1-10) weighing deadliness, civilian danger, event frequency, geographic spread, and trend direction
- GET /api/conflicts returns GeoJSON with severity scores
- GET /api/news returns GDELT articles with average tone
- Supabase caching layer for events and AI blurbs
- 35 tests covering models, severity algorithm, and API endpoints

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add test CI workflow and fix Zenodo API v2 links shape (#7)

CI integration:
- Add .github/workflows/test.yml: runs npm run test:coverage on push/PR
  to main when src/, test/, package*.json, or tsconfig.json change;
  uploads coverage artifact; generates GitHub Actions step summary
- Add .c8rc.json: c8 coverage config (include build/src/**/*.js,
  reporters: text, lcov, json-summary, html)
- Add scripts/test-summary.js: reads coverage-summary.json and writes
  a markdown coverage table to GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
- Add test/README.md: documents test structure, how to run locally,
  network-dependent skip pattern, and how to add new tests

Bug fix (found by running tests):
- Zenodo REST API v2 uses links.self_html instead of links.html;
  update ZenodoRecord and ZenodoDeposition interfaces to include both
  (self_html preferred, html as fallback) and fix the two call sites
  in src/index.ts that build the url field in tool responses

All 19 tests now pass (was 18/19 before the fix).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Orphan check: Empty inbox/ and archive/ dirs are by design (dropboxes/archives)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: suppress ReqnRoll step trace output in CI

Add reqnroll.json with traceSuccessfulSteps and traceTimings
disabled. Combined with the existing PreTest hook that sets
--verbosity quiet on the build server, this eliminates the
verbose Given/When/Then output from CI logs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: edge extractor tests + register test/meta (WAVE-0)

- Create test/meta/ directory with test-edge-extractor.lua (47 tests, 7 suites)
- Add test/meta to test_dirs in run-tests.lua
- Add source_to_tests mapping: scripts/mutation-edge-check.lua -> meta
- Document test/meta falls in 'other' CI shard

Suites: File scanning (7), Sandbox loading (7), Edge extraction (17),
Broken edge detection (5), Dynamic flagging (2), CLI output (5),
Integration sanity (4).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add container and docker-compose configuration (#4)

- Add multi-stage Dockerfile (node:20-slim, non-root user, healthcheck)
- Add docker-compose.yml with ZENODO_BASE_URL and ZENODO_API_KEY env vars
- Add .dockerignore to keep build context lean
- Add .github/workflows/docker.yml to build and publish to ghcr.io
  on push to main and on releases (linux/amd64 and linux/arm64)
- Add docs/DOCKER.md with usage guide and MCP client config examples
- Update docs/_sidebar.md to include Docker page

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
